column8: Establishment of Dies and Mould Laboratory
column11: The establishment of a Dies and Mould Technology Laboratory is crucial for bridging the skills gap in the manufacturing sector by providing students and professionals with essential hands-on experience in designing, manufacturing, and maintaining dies and moulds. This practical training is vital for fostering innovation and enhancing the competitiveness of industries that rely on precision tooling, such as automotive, electronics, and medical devices. Furthermore, such a laboratory serves as a hub for research and development, enabling advancements in materials, manufacturing processes, and quality control, which can lead to more efficient and cost-effective production. By supporting SMEs and facilitating industry-academia collaboration, a Dies and Mould Technology Laboratory plays a significant role in driving economic growth and technological progress.
column13: A Dies and Mould Technology Laboratory is critically needed to address the significant demand for skilled professionals in the precision manufacturing sector. This specialized facility provides a vital hands-on learning environment where students and technicians can gain practical expertise in the intricate processes of designing, fabricating, and maintaining dies and moulds, which are fundamental to mass production across various industries like automotive, electronics, and consumer goods. Without such a laboratory, the gap between theoretical knowledge and practical application widens, hindering the development of a competent workforce capable of meeting industry standards for quality, efficiency, and innovation. Therefore, establishing this laboratory is essential for cultivating a skilled talent pipeline, driving technological advancements in tooling, and ultimately bolstering the competitiveness and economic contribution of the manufacturing industry.
column14: Graduates of a Dies and Mould Technology program are expected to emerge with a robust skillset encompassing the design, manufacturing, and maintenance of dies and moulds, utilizing advanced CAD/CAM software and operating precision machinery like CNC equipment. They should possess a solid understanding of material science, enabling them to select appropriate materials and comprehend the effects of heat treatment, alongside expertise in quality control and inspection to ensure adherence to strict industry tolerances. Furthermore, graduates are anticipated to be adept problem-solvers, capable of troubleshooting manufacturing issues and performing essential maintenance and repair tasks to optimize tooling performance and longevity. A foundational knowledge of various manufacturing processes, coupled with an awareness of safety protocols and environmental considerations, completes their preparedness for roles in die/mould design, fabrication, and maintenance within the competitive manufacturing landscape.

column8: COMPREHENSIVE UPGRADING AND MODERNIZATION OF THE TED BUILDING FOR POWER PLANT TECHNOLOGY, WELDING TECHNOLOGY, HVAC/R TECHNOLOGY, AUTOMOTIVE TCHNOLOGY AND CIVIL TECHNOLOGY LABORATORIES
column11: The Modernization and Upgrading of the TED Building aims to transform the facility into a more functional, safe, and industry-relevant training environment capable of supporting advanced technical instruction and laboratory activities. By improving workshop spaces, upgrading electrical and ventilation systems, and enhancing the overall learning environment, the project will strengthen the University’s capacity to produce competent and industry-ready graduates. Ultimately, this initiative supports the University’s mission of delivering high-quality technology education while reinforcing its role as a leading institution in technical and engineering training.
column13:
The Technology Education (TED) Building of the Technological University of the Philippines (TUP) has long served as a vital facility for instruction, skills training, and academic development. Constructed during the American period, the building stands as one of the oldest infrastructures within the համալսity. While it holds historical significance, its current physical condition raises serious concerns regarding safety, functionality, and suitability for modern educational use.

Over the years, the building has experienced significant structural deterioration due to age and prolonged use. Notably, portions of the ceiling have been continuously deteriorating and, in some instances, falling apart, posing a direct risk to the safety of students, faculty, and staff. Despite these hazards, classes and laboratory activities continue to be conducted in the facility due to the absence of an alternative location for the TED shops and laboratories.

The present condition of the building no longer aligns with the standards of a safe and conducive learning environment. It compromises not only the physical well-being of its occupants but also the quality of instruction and training being delivered. Immediate intervention is therefore necessary.

While the exterior façade of the TED Building reflects its historical character and may be preserved for heritage purposes, it is imperative that comprehensive interior renovation be undertaken. The proposed renovation aims to reinforce structural components, replace deteriorated ceilings, upgrade utilities, and improve overall safety conditions. This will ensure that the facility becomes compliant with current safety standards and capable of supporting effective teaching and learning processes.

Ultimately, the renovation of the TED Building is not merely an infrastructure improvement but a critical investment in the safety, welfare, and academic success of all stakeholders. It demonstrates the university’s commitment to providing a secure, functional, and quality learning environment while respecting the historical value of its existing structures.

In view of the foregoing, the immediate approval and implementation of the renovation of the TED Building is strongly justified and highly recommended.
column14:
Enhanced Safety and Risk Reduction
The renovation will address critical structural issues, particularly the deteriorating ceilings and interior components, thereby significantly reducing the risk of accidents, injuries, and potential liabilities. It ensures a safe environment for students, faculty, and all building occupants.

Improved Learning Environment
Upgrading the interior conditions will create a more conducive space for teaching, learning, and hands-on training. A safe, well-maintained facility enhances student focus, engagement, and overall academic performance.

Compliance with Safety and Building Standards
The project will bring the TED Building in line with current structural, safety, and occupational standards, ensuring compliance with government regulations and audit requirements.

Increased Institutional Credibility
Providing safe and upgraded facilities reflects the university’s commitment to quality education and responsible infrastructure management, strengthening its reputation among stakeholders, accrediting bodies, and partner institutions.

Enhanced Faculty and Student Productivity
A rehabilitated environment supports better delivery of instruction, improves morale, and allows both faculty and students to perform their tasks more efficiently and effectively.

Support for Modernized Training and Equipment Use
Improved interiors will better accommodate updated tools, machinery, and technologies essential for technical and engineering education programs.

column8: Renovation of the Power Plant Engineering Technology Building for Enhanced Energy Sustainability and Backup Power Integration
column11:
This project aims to position the Technological University of the Philippines Manila (TUP Manila) as a forward-looking, energy-resilient institution through the implementation of a hybrid power system that combines renewable and conventional energy sources. Specifically, it seeks to reduce the university’s dependence on the conventional power grid by harnessing clean energy from photovoltaic (PV) solar systems and small-scale wind turbines, thereby promoting energy independence and sustainability. At the same time, the project aims to ensure uninterrupted campus operations by incorporating a diesel generator as a backup power source, guaranteeing continuous electricity supply during outages or periods of low renewable energy generation.

Furthermore, the project intends to lower long-term operational costs associated with electricity consumption, allowing the university to reallocate financial resources toward academic enhancement, research development, and facility improvement. It also supports environmental responsibility by reducing carbon emissions and aligning the institution with sustainable energy practices. In addition, the proposed system will strengthen the university’s resilience against power disruptions, ensuring the continuity of instruction, laboratory activities, and administrative services.

Beyond its operational and economic benefits, the project is also designed to serve as a hands-on learning facility for students, particularly those in Power Plant Engineering Technology and related programs. The integration of solar, wind, and diesel systems within the campus will provide students with real-world exposure to hybrid energy systems, energy management, and modern power generation technologies. This transforms the university itself into a living laboratory, enhancing practical learning, research opportunities, and technical competency. Ultimately, this initiative not only improves energy reliability and sustainability but also reinforces TUP Manila’s role as a leading institution in engineering and technological education, making it a strategic and high-impact investment for university management to pursue.
column13:
The implementation of a campus-wide hybrid energy system at the Technological University of the Philippines Manila (TUP Manila) is both a strategic and necessary initiative to address critical operational, economic, and academic challenges currently faced by the university. At present, the institution relies heavily on the conventional electrical grid, which is vulnerable to power interruptions, voltage fluctuations, and increasing energy costs. These issues disrupt academic activities, laboratory operations, and administrative functions, ultimately affecting the overall efficiency and quality of education delivered by the university.

In addition, the current facilities used by the Power Plant Engineering Technology (PPET) program are no longer adequate to support safe and effective learning. The existing building presents multiple safety hazards, including structural concerns such as falling debris from the ceiling and deteriorating infrastructure. These conditions pose significant risks to students, faculty, and equipment, limiting the conduct of hands-on laboratory activities and restricting the full development of technical competencies. The absence of a safe and modern laboratory environment hinders experiential learning, which is essential in engineering and technology education.

This project directly addresses these concerns by proposing the development of a sustainable, reliable, and safe energy infrastructure integrated across the campus. The inclusion of renewable energy sources such as solar and wind systems, complemented by a diesel generator for backup, ensures continuous power supply while reducing dependency on the grid. At the same time, the modernization of facilities associated with this system provides an opportunity to establish safe, well-equipped laboratory spaces where students can engage in practical learning without exposure to physical hazards.

Moreover, the project responds to the growing need for institutions to adopt environmentally responsible practices and energy-efficient solutions. By transitioning to a hybrid renewable energy system, TUP Manila can significantly reduce its carbon footprint while serving as a model for sustainable campus development.

Ultimately, this project is needed not only to improve energy reliability and reduce operational costs but also to ensure the safety of students and enhance the quality of technical education. It provides a long-term solution that addresses both infrastructure deficiencies and energy challenges, making it a critical investment for the university’s future.
column14:
The implementation of the proposed hybrid energy system and facility improvement at the Technological University of the Philippines Manila (TUP Manila) is expected to deliver significant benefits across academic, operational, economic, and environmental aspects of the university.

The project will provide a safer and more conducive learning environment, particularly for students under the Power Plant Engineering Technology program. By addressing existing structural hazards and upgrading laboratory facilities, students and faculty can conduct experiments and hands-on activities without exposure to risks such as falling debris and unsafe infrastructure. This improvement will enhance the overall quality of instruction and promote a culture of safety within the campus.

It will also ensure a reliable and uninterrupted power supply, minimizing disruptions in classes, laboratory work, and administrative operations. The integration of renewable energy sources with a backup diesel generator will strengthen the university’s energy security and operational continuity, even during power outages.

In terms of academic advancement, the project will transform the campus into a living laboratory, allowing students to gain practical experience with solar, wind, and hybrid power systems. This will significantly enhance their technical competencies, making them more prepared for real-world applications and industry demands.

Economically, the university will benefit from reduced electricity costs over time through the use of renewable energy, enabling better allocation of financial resources toward research, facility development, and academic programs. Additionally, the project supports increased capacity and improved facility utilization, as upgraded infrastructure can accommodate more students and more advanced laboratory activities.

From an environmental perspective, the project promotes sustainability and reduced carbon emissions, aligning the university with global and national initiatives for clean energy adoption. This not only enhances the institution’s reputation but also demonstrates its commitment to environmental responsibility.

Overall, the project will result in improved learning spaces, enhanced safety, greater operational efficiency, increased institutional resilience, and stronger academic and research capabilities, making it a highly beneficial and forward-looking investment for TUP Manila.

column8: Rehabilitation of COE Building
column11:
The proposed Rehabilitation of the College of Engineering (COE) Building aims to upgrade, repair, and modernize the existing academic facilities to ensure a safe, resilient, and technologically responsive learning environment. The project will include structural repairs, architectural improvements, electrical and mechanical system upgrades, ICT infrastructure enhancement, laboratory rehabilitation, and improvement of safety and accessibility features.

The rehabilitation initiative is aligned with the Technological University of the Philippines Land Use Development and Infrastructure Plan (LUDIP), which serves as the University's blueprint for infrastructure development to support quality instruction, research, and extension services.

Specifically, the project may include:

• Structural assessment and repair of deteriorated building components
• Rehabilitation of classrooms, laboratories, and faculty rooms
• Upgrading of electrical, lighting, and ventilation systems
• Improvement of fire safety, disaster resilience, and emergency systems
• Modernization of engineering laboratories to support emerging technologies
• Installation of energy-efficient and sustainable building features
• Improvement of student learning spaces aligned with outcomes-based education
• Compliance with accessibility standards and building safety codes

The rehabilitation will ensure that the COE building remains functional, safe, and responsive to the increasing demands of engineering education and research.
column13:
The proposed rehabilitation of the College of Engineering (COE) Building is necessary to address the deteriorating condition of existing facilities, the need for modernization of engineering laboratories, and the increasing demand for safe, functional, and technology-enabled learning environments. As one of the major academic units of the Technological University of the Philippines, the College of Engineering requires updated infrastructure to effectively support instruction, research, and extension functions consistent with the University's mandate to provide advanced technological education.

Over time, continuous use of the building has resulted in structural wear, outdated electrical and mechanical systems, inadequate laboratory facilities, and limited learning spaces that no longer fully support current engineering education requirements. Without rehabilitation, these conditions may affect safety, instructional effectiveness, research productivity, and the overall learning environment of students and faculty.

Furthermore, the rapid advancement of engineering fields such as electronics, microelectronics, artificial intelligence, automation, and digital systems requires modern laboratories and upgraded facilities to maintain program relevance and competitiveness. The absence of improved facilities may limit the University’s ability to produce industry-ready graduates and conduct high-impact research.

The project also addresses the need to comply with safety standards, building regulations, and disaster resilience requirements. Infrastructure improvements will help ensure structural safety, fire protection readiness, energy efficiency, and accessibility compliance, thereby protecting university stakeholders and institutional assets.

Additionally, the rehabilitation supports the University’s Land Use Development and Infrastructure Plan (LUDIP), which emphasizes that infrastructure development is essential to improve the delivery of quality education, strengthen research capability, and provide a conducive learning environment for stakeholders.

If not implemented, the continued deterioration of facilities may result in higher future repair costs, disruption of academic activities, safety risks, and reduced institutional competitiveness. Thus, the rehabilitation of the COE Building is necessary to ensure that the University can continue to deliver quality engineering education and fulfill its mandate as a premier technological institution.
column14:
The rehabilitation of the College of Engineering (COE) Building is expected to generate significant academic, operational, and institutional benefits that will strengthen the delivery of engineering education, research capability, and student services of the Technological University of the Philippines.

1. Improved Learning Environment
The project will provide modernized classrooms and laboratories that support effective teaching and learning, resulting in improved student engagement, better learning outcomes, and enhanced academic performance.

2. Enhanced Safety and Structural Integrity
The rehabilitation will address structural deterioration, electrical hazards, and other safety concerns, ensuring compliance with building, fire safety, and disaster resilience standards, thereby protecting students, faculty, and staff.

3. Increased Functional Capacity of Facilities
Upgraded spaces will allow more efficient utilization of classrooms and laboratories, helping accommodate increasing student enrollment and improving scheduling efficiency of academic activities.

4. Strengthened Engineering Laboratory Capability
Improved laboratory infrastructure will support advanced experimentation, design activities, and applied research in areas such as electronics, microelectronics, automation, artificial intelligence, and emerging engineering technologies.

5. Improved Research Productivity
Better facilities will enable faculty and students to conduct higher-quality research, support innovation initiatives, and strengthen collaboration with industry and government partners.

6. Support for Accreditation and Quality Assurance
Modern facilities will help ensure compliance with CHED standards, accreditation requirements, and outcomes-based education frameworks necessary for maintaining the quality of engineering programs.

7. Energy Efficiency and Sustainability Improvements
Upgraded electrical systems, lighting, and ventilation may reduce energy consumption, lower maintenance costs, and support sustainable campus development initiatives.

8. Improved Student Experience and Well-Being
Better ventilation, lighting, accessibility features, and learning spaces will improve student comfort, morale, and overall academic experience.

9. Reduced Maintenance Costs
Timely rehabilitation will prevent further deterioration of the building, reducing the need for costly emergency repairs and extending the service life of the facility.

10. Strengthened Institutional Competitiveness
Modern engineering facilities will enhance the University’s capability to produce globally competitive graduates, attract research funding, and strengthen partnerships with industry and government agencies.

column8: Business Entrepreneurship Incubation Room
column11: The proposed Business Entrepreneurship Incubation Room Project aims to establish a dedicated, innovation-driven space within the College of Management and Multidisciplinary Studies (CMMS) Building that will support the academic, practical, and entrepreneurial development of the students. This facility will serve as a dynamic hub for ideation, collaboration, business experimentation, and startup incubation. Furthermore, the incubation room aligns with the institutional goal of producing globally competitive graduates equipped with 21st-century skills. It supports the thrust of the College to integrate practical application with academic instruction, thereby strengthening the link between education, industry, and community engagement. The facility may also serve as a platform for partnerships with local businesses, industry experts, and alumni entrepreneurs who can provide mentorship, training, and potential funding opportunities.
column13: The project is anchored on the principle that effective entrepreneurship education requires access to resources, mentorship, and a conducive environment for innovation. By providing essential tools such as workspaces, digital resources, presentation equipment, and collaborative areas, the incubation room will enhance students’ ability to transform ideas into viable business ventures. It will also foster creativity, critical thinking, risk-taking, and problem-solving, key competencies for future entrepreneurs.

column8: Procurement of Chemistry Laboratory Equipment (to meet the minimum requirements of CMO No.47, series of 2017)
column11:
During the recent visit conducted by the Office of Programs and Standards Development (OPSD), several critical recommendations were raised regarding the current condition and adequacy of the laboratory facilities of the College of Science (COS).

The evaluators noted that a number of essential laboratory equipment and instruments required for laboratory courses, as stipulated in CHED Memorandum Order No. 47, s. 2017 (CMO 47, s. 2017) for the Bachelor of Science in Chemistry—currently being used as a reference in evaluating the Bachelor of Applied Science in Laboratory Technology (BASLT) program—are not yet available in the laboratories. These include, but are not limited to, a refractometer, laboratory shaker, electrophoresis apparatus, melting point apparatus, polymerase chain reaction (PCR) apparatus, polarimeter, tensiometer, viscometer, vortex mixer, constant-volume and pressure, calorimeter, portable FTIR and SEM. Consequently, there is an urgent need to procure additional laboratory equipment and instruments to ensure compliance with the minimum requirements prescribed in the CMO.

 Furthermore, based on the results of the evaluation conducted by the Commission on Higher Education (CHED), the application for the issuance of the Certificate of Program Compliance (COPC) cannot be favorably considered at present. The institution was likewise advised not to operate the program without the appropriate authority from the Commission. In view of this, the acquisition of the required laboratory equipment and instruments becomes imperative should the Technological University of the Philippines pursue the restructuring and eventual implementation of the BASLT-related program.

In response to these findings and recommendations, the College of Science respectfully proposes the procurement of the aforementioned laboratory equipment and the improvement of laboratory facilities. These initiatives aim to ensure compliance with regulatory standards, facilitate the issuance of the COPC, strengthen instructional delivery, and support the continuous improvement of laboratory-based academic programs.
column13:
To procure the required laboratory equipment and upgrade the laboratory facilities of the College of Science (COS) in order to comply with the standards prescribed in CHED Memorandum Order No. 47, s. 2017 and the recommendations of the Office of Programs and Standards Development. This initiative is essential to ensure institutional readiness should the Technological University of the Philippines pursue the restructuring and eventual implementation of the Bachelor of Applied Science in Laboratory Technology (BASLT)–related program (i.e., BS Chemistry)

Specific Objectives:

1.	To procure essential laboratory equipment and instruments required for laboratory courses, including but not limited to a refractometer, laboratory shaker, electrophoresis apparatus, melting point apparatus, polymerase chain reaction (PCR) apparatus, polarimeter, tensiometer, viscometer, vortex mixer, constant pressure calorimeter, constant-volume calorimeter, portable Fourier transform infrared spectroscopy (FTIR), and scanning electron microscope (SEM). 

2.	To strengthen the laboratory capabilities of the institution in support of the restructuring and future implementation of the BASLT- related program at the Technological University of the Philippines.

3.	To enhance the quality of laboratory instruction, practical training, and research opportunities for students through the provision of adequate and updated laboratory equipment and facilities.
column14:
strengthen the laboratory capabilities of the institution in support of the restructuring and future implementation of the BASLT- related program at the Technological University of the Philippines. The chemistry equipment suggested will meet the minimum requirement of CMO No.47, series of 2017.

enhance the quality of laboratory instruction, practical training, and research opportunities for students through the provision of adequate and updated laboratory equipment and facilities.

column8: Multi pupose (parking/offices/commercial spaces) building
column11:
The development shall be a multi-storey, mixed-use facility designed to optimize land utilization while addressing the growing infrastructural demands of the University. The building is envisioned to include:
Ground and Lower Floors:
Structured parking facility for students, faculty, staff, and visitors
Traffic management system and security provisions
Intermediate Floors:
Commercial spaces (e.g., food outlets, bookstores, service centers, and partner-operated facilities)
Spaces for industry linkage offices and innovation hubs
Upper Floors:
Multi-purpose halls and function rooms for academic, research, and university events
Flexible learning spaces and conference facilities
The facility shall adopt modern, sustainable, and resilient building standards, incorporating:
Energy-efficient systems
Disaster-resilient structural design
Smart building technologies
Accessibility-compliant features
The project may be implemented through government funding

The proposed Multi-Purpose Building is anchored on the University’s commitment to modernization, resource optimization, and financial sustainability, while supporting its mandate as a premier state university in technology and applied sciences.
column13:
1. Addressing Critical Infrastructure Gaps
TUP currently faces limited parking capacity, insufficient multi-purpose venues, and fragmented commercial services, which constrain campus operations and negatively affect stakeholder experience. This project directly addresses these gaps by consolidating essential functions into a single, efficient facility.

2. Efficient Land Utilization
Given the limited available land within TUP campuses, vertical development through a mixed-use structure maximizes space efficiency while preserving open areas for future academic expansion.

3. Enhancement of Student and Faculty Welfare
The inclusion of accessible commercial spaces and service areas will improve daily campus life by providing:
Affordable food options
Academic support services
Convenient access to essential goods

4. Strengthening Industry Linkages and Innovation Ecosystem
Dedicated spaces within the building may serve as innovation hubs, incubation centers, and industry collaboration zones, supporting TUP’s thrust toward research commercialization, entrepreneurship, and internationalization.

5. Revenue Generation and Financial Sustainability
The commercial component of the project presents an opportunity for internally generated income (IGI) through leasing and partnerships, which can:
Support university operations
Fund academic and research initiatives
Reduce reliance on government subsidy

6. Support to Institutional Events and Academic Functions
The multi-purpose halls will provide dedicated venues for university-wide activities, including:
Academic conferences
Cultural and student events
Extension and community engagement programs

7. Alignment with Strategic and National Development Goals
The project aligns with:
TUP’s vision of becoming a globally competitive technological university
CHED directives on infrastructure modernization and quality education delivery
National goals on sustainable and smart campus development

Mezzanine Area eXpansion for Structured Planning and Area Capacity Enhancement
column11: a
column12: RQAT, Institutional Infrastructure Improvement and Operational Efficiency Enhancement, including Space Optimization and Document Storage Management (ETEEAP Office)Institutional Infrastructure Improvement and Operational Efficiency Enhancement, including Space Optimization and Document Storage Management (ETEEAP Office)
column13: The proposed M.A.X. S.P.A.C.E. (Mezzanine Area eXpansion for Strategic Planning and Area Capacity Enhancement) project is essential to address the growing space limitations within the ETEEAP Office, driven by the increasing number of applicants, assessment activities, and document processing requirements. The current office layout is no longer sufficient to support efficient workflow, resulting in congestion, limited workstations, and challenges in managing and securing voluminous records.  This project will maximize vertical space through the development of a mezzanine area, enabling the creation of additional workspaces, dedicated assessment areas, and organized document storage systems. It will significantly improve operational efficiency, enhance records management, and provide a more conducive working and service environment for staff, assessors, and clients.  While primarily an infrastructure and operational enhancement initiative, the project also contributes to the university’s compliance with Recognition of Quality Assurance in Teaching and Learning (RQAT) standards by strengthening support systems, improving administrative processes, and ensuring systematic records management.  By optimizing space utilization, the university can ensure sustainable and efficient operations of the ETEEAP Office while reinforcing its commitment to quality service delivery and continuous improvement.
column14:
The implementation of the M.A.X. S.P.A.C.E. (Mezzanine Area eXpansion for Strategic Planning and Area Capacity Enhancement) project will provide significant benefits to the ETEEAP Office and the university as a whole. It will result in improved space utilization by maximizing vertical areas, leading to increased workspace capacity for staff and assessors. The project will enhance operational efficiency by reducing congestion and streamlining workflow processes.

Additionally, it will strengthen document storage and records management through organized, secure, and accessible filing systems, ensuring compliance with audit and quality assurance requirements. The creation of dedicated areas for assessment, meetings, and administrative functions will improve service delivery and client experience. Overall, the project will contribute to a more professional, safe, and conducive working environment while supporting institutional growth and continuous quality improvement.

column8: Facility Renovation of Graphic Arts And Printing Technology Departmemt
column11:
Renovation of GAPT Department Facilities (Room 201 and Faculty Areas)
Project Description:
The Renovation of GAPT Department Facilities aims to rehabilitate and improve the current condition of Room 201, faculty room, and surrounding areas within the GAPT Building. The project is initiated to address existing maintenance concerns such as damaged doors, broken water faucets, and general deterioration of facilities that affect daily operations and the overall learning environment.
The renovation will include the repair and replacement of defective fixtures, particularly the broken water faucet in the faculty room and the damaged door in Room 201. Additional improvements will cover repainting of walls, minor structural repairs, and enhancement of ventilation and lighting systems to ensure a safer and more comfortable space for students, faculty members, and staff.
Furthermore, the project will address concerns related to sanitation and proper waste management within the premises, including the improvement of garbage disposal practices around the building. This initiative supports the department’s goal of maintaining a clean, organized, and professional academic environment.
The renovation will be implemented in coordination with the Auxiliary Services Office and in compliance with institutional safety and maintenance standards.
Project Objectives:
To repair the broken water faucet in the faculty room
To restore and fix the damaged door in Room 201
To improve sanitation and waste management practices in the GAPT premises
To enhance the safety, functionality, and comfort of the facilities
To maintain a conducive learning and working environment
Scope of Work:
Replacement/repair of broken water faucet (faculty room)
Repair and rehabilitation of Room 201 door
Repainting and wall touch-ups
Inspection and minor repair of electrical and lighting fixtures
Plumbing system check and maintenance
Cleaning and organization of surrounding areas
Implementation of proper waste disposal measures
Expected Outcomes:
Fully functional and safe facility components
Improved cleanliness and sanitation in the building
Enhanced comfort for faculty, staff, and students
Better maintenance practices within the department
A more conducive and professional learning environment

column8: IRTC Research Faculty Room
column11: The establishment of a centralized Research Faculty Room that brings together faculty members from IMCED, PEED, MEED, and CEMD aims to enhance collaboration, efficiency, and research productivity within the institution. By providing a shared workspace, it encourages interdisciplinary interaction, facilitates the exchange of ideas and best practices, and strengthens coordination in research activities. This initiative will also allow the existing areas currently occupied by faculty to be repurposed as research hubs and lecture rooms for training sessions. Overall, it supports better mentoring, resource sharing, and project development, contributing to higher-quality outputs such as publications, innovations, and funded research while fostering a unified and dynamic research culture.
column13: The proposed centralized Research Faculty Room is justified by the need to optimize space utilization, improve collaboration, and enhance overall research efficiency within the institution. Currently, faculty members are dispersed across different areas, which limits interaction, slows coordination, and underutilizes opportunities for interdisciplinary work. Consolidating IMCED, PEED, MEED, and CEMD faculty into one shared space will streamline communication, strengthen mentoring and supervision, and foster a more cohesive research environment. At the same time, this arrangement enables the conversion of their former spaces into dedicated research hubs and lecture rooms for training sessions, thereby maximizing institutional resources and supporting both research and extension functions.
column14: Streamline communication, strengthen mentoring and supervision, and foster a more cohesive research environment. At the same time, this arrangement enables the conversion of their former spaces into dedicated research hubs and lecture rooms for training sessions, thereby maximizing institutional resources and supporting both research and extension functions.

column8: PROJECT TECHNIX : Technology and Engineering for Computing and Simulations, Hardware, Networking, Innovation, & eXperiments.
column11:
Technology and Engineering for Computing and Simulations, Hardware, Networking, Innovation, & eXperiments This facility will serve as an immersive environment for professionals/partners to gain practical experience in key areas such as web and mobile development, the Internet of Things (IoT), cybersecurity, robotics and other engineering software. Supports different departments in IRTC such as IMCED, PEED, MEED, and CEMD. It consists of two dedicated laboratory rooms: the Simulation and Training Laboratory and the Cybersecurity and Network Laboratory.

The proposed budget for TECHNIX is ₱16,443,597.36, covering the development, infrastructure, and necessary equipment to enhance hands-on training and research capabilities in these specialized fields.
column12: Micro-credential Provider, Training Provider, CPD Provider, and IGP
column13:
The establishment and continuous enhancement of the Integrated Research and Training Center (IRTC) at the Technological University of the Philippines (TUP) is justified by the growing demand for industry-relevant skills, advanced technological training, and research-driven innovation in the country. As highlighted in the TECHNIX proposal, IRTC serves as a critical platform for bridging the gap between theoretical knowledge and practical application through state-of-the-art simulation laboratories under emerging technology, cybersecurity and networking facilities, and hands-on training in emerging fields such as IoT, artificial intelligence, and robotics. 
It supports national priorities such as the Continuing Professional Development (CPD) requirement under Republic Act No. 10912 and aligns with the National Cybersecurity and emerging plan, ensuring that professionals remain globally competitive and capable of addressing evolving digital threats. By fostering collaboration with industry partners, government agencies, and academic units, IRTC not only strengthens workforce readiness but also contributes to innovation, economic development, and technological resilience, positioning TUP as a leader in advanced engineering education and applied research.
column14: The establishment of the TUP Integrated Research and Training Center (IRTC) is expected to yield significant benefits, including the creation of an improved and technology-enabled learning environment that enhances hands-on training and simulation-based education. It will strengthen safety and security through modern infrastructure, reliable power systems, and integrated cybersecurity measures, ensuring both physical and digital protection. The facility will also increase institutional capacity by accommodating more students, professionals, and industry partners, while supporting diverse training programs in emerging technologies such as IoT, cybersecurity, and robotics. Furthermore, it will enhance research productivity, promote innovation, and improve collaboration with government and industry stakeholders, ultimately producing highly skilled, industry-ready graduates and professionals who can contribute to national development and global competitiveness.

1. Provision of concrete pathway from the access bridge going to the building for easy access of faculty and students.
2. Concreting of TUP grounds in the TUP Quezon Campus in Brgy. Concepcion, Lopez, Quezon.
3. Rehabilitation of the bridge (bridge not only for pedestrians but also for  vehicle) for easy access to the TUP Concepcion campus.

column11:
1. The concrete pathway is needed for the easy access of faculty and students from the existing metal bridge going to the building in the TUP Quezon in Brgy. Concepcion. The pathway will serve as an easy access (most especially during rainy season) to the main grounds of faculty and students from the existing bridge.
2. The concreting of grounds in front of the building will enable the faculty and students to convene easily during assembly, flag raising, etc., and can be used also for PE classes.
3. The existing pedestrian metal bridge is now in need of rehabilitation. Wear and tear caused by the elements can be seen from its metal plates. Apart from its rehabilitation, a provision for its improvement to change of its structure for vehicles (motorcycle, light vehicles) to access the TUP Quezon Concepcion grounds.
4. The rehabilitation of electrical wirings in the second floor of the existing building is also needed. As of this time, the second floor is not yet energized.
5. Repainting of the existing building is also needed.
column13: All of the proposed projects are needed to pass the RQAT. The projects are also necessary  for the operation of the campus and for the eventual transfer of TUP Quezon from Brgy. Villahermosa to Brgy. Concepcion.
